The Chamber of Commerce is out
on big hunt for new members and
new strength and to this end Chair
man F. B. Wright of the member
ship committee has started Wig
orous campaign among the business
men of the city to whom he has is
sued the following stirring appeal:
"The board Of directors of the
Spokane Chamber Of Commerce Is
making a strenuous effort to in
crease the membership, and has re-
quested me to make q special ap
peal to you to poin the Chamber OF
Commerce and give them your
moral and financial support ana
help build up our great Inland EN-
pire.


"You are we'll aware of the fact
that the Portland Exposition will


bring thousands of people to the
northwest. The railroad companies


have given exceedingly low rates
and have made stop-over privileges
to Spokane, so the majority OF the


people who go to Portland will
